front: Poem: epic in Kalevala-esque trochaic tetrameter based on Ojibwe legends from Schoolcraft; hero weds Minnehaha "By the shores of Gitche Gumee"
Poem: hero fasts seven days, wrestles Mondamin — first corn grows from Mondamin's grave
Poem: opens "This is the forest primeval" in dactylic hexameter; title character separated from Gabriel Lajeunesse during Acadian expulsion
Poem: "one if by land, two if by sea" — signal from Old North Church; narrated by the Landlord in a collection set in Sudbury
Poem: "Life is real! Life is earnest!" — urges reader to "leave behind footprints on the sands of time" and "Learn to labour and to wait"
Poem: love triangle of Miles Standish, John Alden, and Priscilla Mullins — "Why don't you speak for yourself, John?"
Work: 1863 collection framed as stories by tavern patrons in Sudbury; includes "Paul Revere's Ride," "The Saga of King Olaf," and introduced "ships that pass in the night"
Poem: youth found frozen at St. Bernard Pass carrying a banner with a "strange device" reading the title Latin word meaning "ever higher"
Poem: title worker stands "under a spreading chestnut tree" — the Cambridge tree later chopped down and made into a chair
Author: wife died in a house fire; grew trademark beard afterward; wrote "The Cross of Snow" 18 years later
